HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF RONALD FOREMAN's Motion To Disqualify David H. Schwartz, Nancy Chung, And The Law Offices Of David H. Schwartz, Inc.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Ronald D. Foreman dba Foreman & Brasso, Attorneys at Law's motion to disqualify Defendants' counsel David H. Schwartz, Nancy Chung, and the Law Offices of David H. Schwartz, Inc. (Schwartz) is denied.

Notice Of Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Dec-29-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. DAVID J. COOK, Esq. of Cook Collection Attorneys, PLC (Tel: 415-989-4730) for Plaintiff Ronald D. Foreman dba Foreman & Brasso, Attorneys at Law;
Atty. DAVID H. SCHWARTZ, Esq. of Law Offices of David H. Schwartz, Inc. (Tel: 415-399.9301) for Defendants Ann Akhromtsev, Daniel Akhromtsev, Danma Consulting.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS ANN AKHROMTSEV, DANIEL AKHROMTSEV, AND DANMA CONSULTING's Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendants' motion to compel arbitration and stay proceedings is denied. The party seeking arbitration has the burden of proving the existence of an arbitration agreement. " 'In determining whether an arbitration agreement applies to a specific dispute, the court may examine only the agreement itself and the complaint filed by the party refusing arbitration.'" (Rice v. Downs (2016) 248 Cal.App.4th 175, 185, quoting Weeks v. Crow (1980) 113 Cal.App.3d 350, 353.) Whether Defendants intend to raise defenses or file cross-claims is of no moment. Here, Defendants' motion is based on an attorney-client retainer agreement that contains a mandatory binding arbitration provision; however, the provision states, "This dispute resolution provision specifically excludes the collection of attorney's fees which may be litigated in a lawsuit and are specifically excluded from any arbitration provision, except as may be mandated by the Business & Professions Code, regulating attorney services." (Akhromtsev Decl. par. 2.) As Defendants admit, "This case is a suit for collection of attorney fees." (Schwartz Decl. par. 2.) Plaintiff's complaint in this action asserts claims for breach of the retainer agreement and for quantum meruit, both of which fall squarely within the exclusion for "collection of attorney's fees." "Although the law favors contracts for arbitration of disputes between parties, there is no policy compelling persons to accept arbitration of controversies which they have not agreed to arbitrate." (Goldman v. SunBridge Healthcare, LLC (2013) 220 Cal.App.4th 1160, 1169.) There is no ambiguity; the dispute is not arbitrable. Per the agreement of the parties, defendants have 15 days to answer the complaint.

Order signed. Both sides waive notice of entry of the Court's order.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)
